summary: Stellar Dark Star sandwich and Keith’s debut on keys
blurb: The show shines with an expansive Dark Star > Sitting On Top Of The World > Dark Star sequence and tight, high‑energy first‑set jams. Jerry delivers soaring leads while Keith Godchaux’s piano/organ is crystal clear.
review: October 21, 1971 at Chicago’s Auditorium Theatre captures the Dead in a period of transition and triumph. The band rolls out a punchy first set – Truckin’, Tennessee Jed and Brown Eyed Women all land with fresh vigor – before launching into one of the night’s high points: an epic Dark Star sandwich bookended by Sitting On Top Of The World. Jerry Garcia is described as an “assassin” on guitar, weaving hypnotic leads that soar over Keith Godchaux’s debut‑season organ and piano, which dominate the mix in glorious clarity on the Miller soundboard capture. Phil Lesh lays down a “terror” of bass, locking in with Bill Kreutzmann’s steady drive, while Bob Weir adds inspired vocals during the Dark Star transition. The recording quality is superb – crystal‑clear SBD that balances keyboards and guitars without dropout – making this one of the most rewarding late‑1971 performances to hear.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four analyzed recordings share uniformly positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the Dark Star > Sitting On Top Of The World > Dark Star sandwich, Keith’s standout keyboard debut, and tight band interplay. No negative comments about performance appear, warranting a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Miller soundboard capture praised as crystal‑clear with excellent balance, especially highlighting Keith’s piano and the Dark Star sandwich.

key_highlights: Epic Dark Star > Sitting On Top Of The World > Dark Star sandwich that drives the second set.
Keith Godchaux’s debut organ/piano shines with crystal‑clear presence throughout the night.
High‑energy first set featuring standout Truckin’, Tennessee Jed and Brown Eyed Women.
song_highlights: Sitting On Top Of The World

band_performance.Jerry: Inspired throughout, delivering soaring leads and hypnotic improvisation, especially on the Dark Star sandwich.
band_performance.Phil: A “terror” on bass with brilliant interplay that underpins the entire show.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals and rhythm guitar rise during the Dark Star transition; vocal duels add extra excitement.
band_performance.Keith: Shines on piano/organ, debuting his Hammond sound; his playing is loud, clear, and adds fresh texture, though sometimes sits forward in the mix.
band_performance.Billy: Provides solid, steady drumming that supports the band's high‑energy flow.

must_listen_sequences: Sitting On Top Of The World
